$comment: Committed Live View streaming performance thresholds (spec §16). Machine-readable; every
threshold carries its measurement metadata (profile, runner, metric, unit, bound, duration,
warmup, repetitions, aggregation). Changing a threshold requires review + evidence (§21) — do
NOT auto-rewrite a baseline to make a regression pass. The soak/drift gates run on every build
(host, deterministic). The device CPU gates are asserted by the Pixel-4 HIL fixture, which is
currently MANUAL (no self-hosted CI runner) — see docs/plans/live-view and tools/hil.
Relative-regression gate only: a dedicated, quiesced runner is required for a HARD absolute
CPU gate (§14.3/§16.4 — a shared cloud runner is too noisy). The vitest bench prints ops/s;
assert-stream-perf.mjs compares against a committed baseline with a tolerance band.

deviceCpu.$comment: Absolute ceilings measured on-device 2026-07-24; asserted by the manual HIL fixture, not a CI gate yet.
